steps.title: Identify the fundamental flaw Text 2 raises
steps.content: Text 2 criticizes studies for lacking “control groups” and “pretraining baselines”—the methodological tools needed to answer one essential question: Did the high performance actually result from training, or would it have happened anyway?

steps.title: Match the objection these safeguards would eliminate
steps.content: Control groups and pretraining baselines directly rule out the objection that participants would show high performance regardless of training—exactly what choice C expresses. A, B, and D address concerns outside Text 2's specific critique. That is the correct answer.
